general.FB1: Students must complete minimum credits per year as specified for their programme.
general.FB2: Major sequences must be completed through 1000, 2000, and 3000 levels.
general.FB3: Students may not register for courses at a higher level without completing prerequisites.
general.FB4: Extended programme students follow augmented first-year curriculum with additional academic development courses.

music_rules.FS1: BMus students must maintain instrument studies throughout all 4 years.
music_rules.FS2: Ensemble participation compulsory for all BMus and DMP students.
music_rules.FS3: BMus Year 4 research report (MUZ4900W or MUZ4901W) compulsory for all streams.
music_rules.FS4: Performance examinations held at end of each year for all instrument courses.
music_rules.FS5: Students must pass instrument course to proceed to next year level.
music_rules.FS6: BMus General stream requires breadth across Western Art Music, Ethnomusicology, and South African Music.
music_rules.FS7: DMP is a 3-year diploma; does not lead directly to Honours without bridging.
music_rules.FS8: BMus Extended (HB034): 5-year version, continuing students only — no new intake 2026.
music_rules.FS9: DMP Extended (HU034): 4-year version, continuing students only.
music_rules.FS10: Instrument allocation subject to availability and audition results.
music_rules.FS11: All BMus/DMP students attend weekly masterclasses.
music_rules.FS12: Repertoire requirements specified per instrument and year level.

fine_art_rules.FBN1: BA(Fine Art) Year 1: Art Practice 1 (FIN1001W) and Visual Culture Studies 1 (FIN1005W) compulsory.
fine_art_rules.FBN2: Year 2: choose 2 studiowork courses from available media.
fine_art_rules.FBN4: Year 4: Fine Art 4 (FIN4015W, 108 credits) is the capstone studio project.
fine_art_rules.FBN5: Theory of Art courses compulsory in years 1-3.
fine_art_rules.FBN6: Humanities electives required in years 1-3 (minimum 1 per year).
fine_art_rules.FBN7: Extended programme (HB064): 5-year version with DOH1005F in Year 1.
fine_art_rules.FBN8: Portfolio review required for progression from Year 2 to Year 3.
fine_art_rules.FBN9: Exhibition at end of Year 4 is part of assessment.

social_work_rules.FBW1: BSW students must complete all field instruction placements (SWK*070*, SWK*075*, SWK*032*, SWK*033*).
social_work_rules.FBW2: Professional registration with SACSSP required upon graduation.
social_work_rules.FBW3: PSY and SOC courses compulsory as supporting disciplines in years 1-3.

screen_production_rules.FB11: BA Screen Production (HB067): FAM1000S compulsory in Year 1.
screen_production_rules.FB12: Screen Production courses (FAM2013F, FAM2014S, FAM3016F, FAM3017S) are sequential.
screen_production_rules.FB13: Portfolio submission required for admission to Screen Production programme.

ppe_rules.FB14: BSocSc(PPE): ECO, PHI, POL all compulsory — cannot drop any of the three disciplines.
ppe_rules.FB15: STA1000S and MAM1010F compulsory in Year 1 as quantitative foundations.

theatre_rules.FGT1: Diploma in Theatre: 3-year programme with Year 3 specialisation choice.
theatre_rules.FGT2: BA(T&P): 4-year degree with 5 specialisations, HEQSF exit level 8.
theatre_rules.FGT3: Performance assessment at end of each year.
theatre_rules.FGT5: TDP1027F (Voice and Movement I) compulsory for all theatre students.
theatre_rules.FGT6: Year 3 specialisation: choose one from Acting, Dance, Performance Making, Applied Performance/Pedagogy, Scenography.
theatre_rules.FGT7: Advanced Diploma in Theatre (HU050): not offered in 2026.
theatre_rules.FGT8: DOH1005F compulsory for Diploma in Theatre Year 1.

extended_programme.DOH_augmenting: Extended programme students take augmenting versions of courses paired with standard equivalents.
extended_programme.pairs_note: Standard and extended course pairs exist at 1000, 2000, and 3000 levels across multiple departments.
